brief_justification: Wrong answer (No vs Yes) - the agent correctly identified the causal structure and do-calculus intervention but assumed Jyka can only be present when Yupt is present, ignoring that Jyka could have a high base rate independent of Yupt's effect; Reasoning goes one level deep with do-calculus application but misses that P(Jyka=1) is not bounded by P(Yupt=1) since Jyka can have its own baseline probability; Minimal self-monitoring - no checking of the assumption that Jyka requires Yupt; No verification or sanity check against the possibility that Jyka's marginal could exceed 11%; Presents conclusion as fairly certain without acknowledging uncertainty about Jyka's conditional probabilities; No alternative interpretations considered for how Jyka's probability might be high despite low Yupt; Derivation chain is shown clearly but built on an unverified assumption
